children.heading: Optional coverages
children.label: l Optional coverages
children.text: The Corporation may offer specific risk protection programs, including protection against prevented planting, wildlife depredation, tree damage and disease, and insect infestation, under such terms and conditions as the Board may determine, except that no program may be undertaken if insurance for the specific risk involved is generally available from private companies.

children.heading: Effect of coverage
children.label: 1 Effect of coverage
children.text: If a policy or plan of insurance offered under this subchapter includes quality loss adjustment coverage, the coverage shall provide for a reduction in the quantity of production of the agricultural commodity considered produced during a crop year, or a similar adjustment, as a result of the agricultural commodity not meeting the quality standards established in the policy or plan of insurance.

children.label: i
children.text: The agricultural commodity is sold on an identity-preserved basis.

children.label: ii
children.text: All quality determinations are made solely by the Federal agency designated to grade or classify the agricultural commodity.

children.label: iii
children.text: All quality determinations are made in accordance with standards published by the Federal agency in the Federal Register.

children.label: iv
children.text: The discount schedules that reflect the reduction in quality of the agricultural commodity are established by the Secretary.

children.heading: Basis for adjustment
children.label: B Basis for adjustment
children.text: Under this paragraph, the Corporation shall set the quality standards below which quality losses will be paid based on the variability of the grade of the agricultural commodity from the base quality for the agricultural commodity.

children.heading: Review
children.label: A Review
children.text: The Corporation shall contract with a qualified person to review the quality loss adjustment procedures of the Corporation so that the procedures more accurately reflect local quality discounts that are applied to agricultural commodities insured under this subchapter.

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: B Procedures
children.text: Effective beginning not later than the 2004 reinsurance year, based on the review, the Corporation shall make adjustments in the procedures, taking into consideration the actuarial soundness of the adjustment and the prevention of fraud, waste, and abuse.

children.heading: Special provisions for malting barley
children.label: 5 Special provisions for malting barley
children.text: The Corporation shall promulgate special provisions under this subsection specific to malting barley, taking into consideration any changes in quality factors, as required by applicable market conditions.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation shall establish procedures to allow insured producers not more than 120 days to settle claims, in accordance with procedures established by the Secretary, involving corn that is determined to have low test weight.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: B Implementation
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Corporation shall implement subparagraph (A) on a regional basis based on market conditions and the interests of producers.February 7, 2014

children.heading: Termination of effectiveness
children.label: C Termination of effectiveness
children.text: The authority provided by this paragraph terminates effective on the date that is 5 years after the date on which subparagraph (A) is implemented.

children.heading: Limitation on multiple benefits for same loss
children.label: n Limitation on multiple benefits for same loss

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), if a producer who is eligible to receive benefits under catastrophic risk protection under subsection (b) is also eligible to receive assistance for the same loss under any other program administered by the Secretary, the producer shall be required to elect whether to receive benefits under this subchapter or under the other program, but not both. A producer who purchases additional coverage under subsection (c) may also receive assistance for the same loss under other programs administered by the Secretary, except that the amount received for the loss under the additional coverage together with the amount received under the other programs may not exceed the amount of the actual loss of the producer.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to emergency loans under subtitle C of the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 1961

children.label: i First 4 crop years

children.label: II
children.text: during each of which a crop on that acreage is insured under subsection (c).

children.heading: De minimis acreage exemption
children.label: B De minimis acreage exemption
children.text: The Secretary shall exempt areas of 5 acres or less from subparagraph (A).

children.label: I
children.text: the crop insurance guarantee shall be determined by using a yield equal to 65 percent of the transitional yield of the producer; and

children.label: II
children.text: the crop insurance premium subsidy provided for the producer under this subchapter, except for coverage authorized pursuant to subsection (b)(1), shall be 50 percentage points less than the premium subsidy that would otherwise apply.

children.heading: Yield substitution
children.label: ii Yield substitution
children.text: During the period native sod acreage is covered by this subsection, a producer may not substitute yields for the native sod.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 3 Application
children.text: This subsection shall only apply to native sod acreage in the States of Minnesota, Iowa, North Dakota, South Dakota, Montana, and Nebraska.

children.heading: Coverage levels by practice
children.label: p Coverage levels by practice
children.text: Beginning with the 2015 crop year, a producer that produces an agricultural commodity on both dry land and irrigated land may elect a different coverage level for each production practice.
